rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (just LGTM/approval), so both candidates trivially satisfy recall. X delivers three concrete, grounded findings with exact file/line and suggested rewrites, including a genuinely useful catch that a sibling doc (README.md:58) still has the stale extra_body pattern this PR fixes elsewhere. Y does impressive deep source-code verification (citing specific vllm-omni line numbers) confirming the PR's technical claims, but its single comment is hedgy and non-actionable, essentially deferring back to the author rather than requesting a change. X's edge in actionability gives it a slight overall advantage.
